solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on exact implementation details like the specific function name `bindToOverlay`, exact condition string `len(mounts) != 1 || mounts[0].Type != "bind"`, exact filter `if o != "rbind"`, and exact call pattern `mounts = bindToOverlay(mounts)`. tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests test_bind_to_overlay_single_bind, test_bind_to_overlay_passthrough, test_bind_to_overlay_multiple, test_parallel_unpack_invokes_bind_to_overlay, test_imports_correct, and test_no_regression_unpack_logic are all pure text-matching (`assert '...' in content`). While test_unit_unpacker, test_build, test_vet, test_go_fmt, and test_go_mod_verify do execute subprocesses, the majority of f2p tests are text-only checks.

test_not_tautological.reason: The test_unit_unpacker test runs `go test -run TestBindToOverlay` which executes actual Go test cases that verify the bindToOverlay function's behavior with reflect.DeepEqual. A stub implementation would fail these Go-level tests. test_build and test_vet also require compilable code.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution exists at /workspace/containerd with solve.sh available. The agent could read solution/solve.sh (if accessible in the container) or simply grep the test file at tests/test_outputs.py to extract every exact string being asserted, then write code matching those strings without understanding the bug.
